message:
style(engine): apply clippy lints and rustfmt across 13 modules and test files

Addresses a sweep of Clippy suggestions and rustfmt formatting across test
code in the engine crate. No behavioral changes; all modifications are in
test functions only.

Clippy fixes applied:
- vec![...] → array literal [...] where heap allocation is unnecessary
  (bayesian_error_recovery, ifc_provenance_index, obligation_integration,
  structural_causal_model)
- assert_eq!(x, true/false) → assert!(x) / assert!(!x) for boolean
  assertions (execution_cell, governance_hooks)
- if x.is_some() { x.as_mut().unwrap() } → if let Some(g) = x.as_mut()
  to eliminate redundant unwrap (semantic_twin)
- Unnecessary .clone() on Copy type removed (obligation_integration phase)
- Added let _ = to suppress unused Result warning (obligation_integration)
- Prefer struct update syntax ..Default::default() over field-by-field
  mutation (incident_replay_bundle, tee_attestation_policy)
- Use std::slice::from_ref(&x) instead of &[x.clone()] for single-element
  slices (incident_replay_bundle merkle functions)

Rustfmt reformatting:
- Long assert_eq! and println! expressions wrapped to 100-col width
  (adversarial_campaign_gate bin, adversarial_campaign_gate_cli test,
  compiler_hotspot_optimization_campaign test, parser_operator_developer
  runbook test)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
